description: Head-to-head: OligoForge offline in-silico-PCR vs a faithful offline Primer-BLAST-equivalent (blastn 2.17.0, same engine Primer-BLAST wraps), benchmarked on a real human transcriptome subset with natural paralog off-targets and on an apicomplexan 18S panel with related-genus off-targets.
ground_truth_definition: Biologically-grounded: a primer primes a subject if its 3' terminus is aligned with a contiguous 3'-match >=15 bp (<=1 mismatch in terminal 5); amplicon if convergent F/R sites within 40-3000 bp. Independent of either tool's heuristic.

human_transcriptome.false_positive_explanation: Both tools flag 2 borderline paralog cross-reactions (ACTG1, YWHAB) that a strict >=15bp-contiguous-3' ground truth treats as non-amplifying. In each the reverse primer has a perfectly 3'-anchored match but with an internal mismatch ~7bp from the 3' end. Both tools err toward CAUTION (warning of possible cross-reactivity) — the correct conservative behavior for assay QC. OligoForge and Primer-BLAST make IDENTICAL calls, including this shared conservative bias.

apicomplexa_18S.probe_nuance: Probe has an isolated binding site in Toxoplasma 18S (pos 1248-1267) but Toxoplasma is NOT amplified by the primer pair (no product), so it produces no qPCR signal. In-assay probe binding is confined to the 3 Plasmodium amplicons. This demonstrates the value of amplicon-context probe evaluation over isolated probe alignment.

headline: OligoForge's offline in-silico-PCR makes IDENTICAL specificity calls to a faithful Primer-BLAST-equivalent on all 11 human-transcriptome pairs (100% concordance) and on the real pan-Plasmodium case. Both achieve sensitivity 100%, specificity 98.5% vs a biologically-grounded ground truth, sharing the same 2 conservative false positives. OligoForge does not outperform Primer-BLAST — it matches it, offline and reproducibly.
